title: Dysoxylum balansanum
taxonomy: Dysoxylum balansanum is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Meliaceae. This family comprises about 360 species of trees and shrubs, commonly known as the mahogany family. Dysoxylum balansanum is further categorized under the genus Dysoxylum, which consists of about 100 species of flowering plants native to Asia. The genus Dysoxylum is known for its timber, which is used for furniture and other wood products. Within the genus Dysoxylum, Dysoxylum balansanum is likely to be further classified into a specific species group based on its physical characteristics.
uses: Dysoxylum balansanum, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. intro: Dysoxylum balansanum, also known as Balansa's Dysoxylum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Dysoxylum balansanum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
